_documentation.purpose: Prevents man-in-the-middle attacks by validating HTTPS certificates against known SPKI hashes
howToObtainPins: 1. Use OpenSSL to get the certificate:

openssl s_client -connect api.openai.com:443 -servername api.openai.com < /dev/null | openssl x509 -pubkey -noout | openssl pkey -pubin -outform der | openssl dgst -sha256 -binary | openssl enc -base64
2. Or use Node.js to extract SPKI from a live connection:
const https = require('https'); const crypto = require('crypto');
3. Add the pin to the 'pins' object with hostname as key
4. Set enforcement.enabled to true to activate pinning
securityNotes: - Pins must be updated when certificates are rotated
- Test thoroughly before enabling in production
- Set enforcement.enabled to false during development

format.enforcement.enabled: Boolean - set to true to enforce pinning
format.enforcement.allowUnpinnedInDevelopment: Boolean - allow unpinned hosts when NODE_ENV=development
